component.name: GIF Forge
component.summary: Record your screen and edit it into an optimized GIF or video

description.p: GIF Forge is a modern Linux screen-capture studio. Record a region, window or screen on Wayland or X11, then refine the result on a timeline — trim, cut, reorder, retime and crop — before exporting an optimized GIF, WebM video or APNG.
It keeps recording simple while adding powerful frame-by-frame editing:

ul.li: Record a region, window or the whole screen
Native Wayland capture via the screen-cast portal, plus X11
Frame-by-frame timeline: delete, duplicate, reorder and reverse frames
Remove duplicate frames and reduce frame count
Adjust per-frame delay and change playback speed
Trim to a selection and crop the frame
Export optimized GIF, WebM or APNG
Save and reopen editable projects, with crash recovery
HiDPI aware, light and dark themes

description.p: Fixes single-frame "wallpaper flashes" in recordings made on X11 under a compositing desktop (seen on GNOME Shell with NVIDIA): glitch frames the capture race produces are now detected and repaired before export. Also fixes GIF exports playing with uneven frame timing (120/80 ms instead of the edited 100 ms delays).

Fixes the capture region showing as opaque grey — and recording a single frozen frame — on X11 sessions without a compositor. The region is now a real see-through hole, so the desktop shows through with or without compositing.

First release: Wayland-first recorder, timeline editor with undo/redo, optimized GIF/WebM/APNG export, editable projects with crash recovery, a localized UI (English/Spanish/French/German/Portuguese), and packages for Flatpak, AppImage, .deb, Arch and Fedora.
